issues.title: Observability don't work

comments.body: I tried, but it still didn't work!

The ctor that includes observability is this one


```
	public OpenAiChatModel(OpenAiApi openAiApi, OpenAiChatOptions defaultOptions, ToolCallingManager toolCallingManager,
			RetryTemplate retryTemplate, ObservationRegistry observationRegistry,
			ToolExecutionEligibilityPredicate toolExecutionEligibilityPredicate) {
		Assert.notNull(openAiApi, "openAiApi cannot be null");
```

when using an autoconfigured `OpenAiChatModel` the correct observability registry is injected.  This is not being set in the builder you are using to create an instance 

```
 OpenAiChatModel chatModel = OpenAiChatModel.builder().openAiApi(openAiApi).defaultOptions(options).build();
```

I would suggest getting an autowired instance of `OpenAiChatModel` and then using the new `mutate` method described here

https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chatclient.html#_multiple_openai_compatible_api_endpoints

and then set the properties that you want which are different.    

Also note, there is a dedicated deepseek model now as the deepseek model is deviating from the openai api.
BTW - https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/3127 is not a duplicate, it is a documentation issue.
Closing as the ObservationRepository instance was not passed in the the builder in your example, that is why it isn't working.
